mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and relaxes financial conditions, so declines in the policy rate predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when the rate is already near the effective lower bound and additional cuts do not change discount-rate expectations.
failure_condition: Rejected if negative 1-month changes in FEDFUNDS do not precede positive 20d SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE marks deteriorating household income and demand, which reduces expected corporate revenues and pushes S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment rises alongside weak payroll growth.
failure_condition: Rejected if unemployment spikes above the trigger but SPX has positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered cases, or if the sign of the effect reverses in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS indicates improving consumer spending and revenue growth for listed firms, which supports positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when sales rise only because of inflation rather than real demand.
failure_condition: Rejected if rising RSAFS does not coincide with positive 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the signal loses predictive power after controlling for CPIAUCSL changes.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO with steady or rising HOUST signals a real-activity rebound and better earnings momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism breaks when output rises but housing remains weak, indicating inventory rebuilding rather than final demand.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered episodes fail to deliver positive 60d SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the effect disappears when HOUST is contracting.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated CPIAUCSL inflation trend combined with a still-low FEDFUNDS rate implies a policy error: real rates are falling behind inflation, which compresses risk appetite and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if inflation acceleration is accompanied by immediate policy tightening.
failure_condition: Rejected if inflation-triggered observations produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the negative effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS is above 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series signals labor-market contraction and weaker household cash flow, which should reduce near-term equity returns over the next week as investors revise growth expectations downward. The effect fails when payroll losses are offset by a simultaneous decline in unemployment.
failure_condition: Rejected if negative PAYEMS changes are not followed by negative 5d SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the rolling 3-year IC is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in HOUST captures housing demand and credit-sensitive spending, which lifts growth expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is invalid when housing starts rise because of policy incentives while broad consumption remains weak.
failure_condition: Rejected if the housing-starts trigger does not correspond to positive 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the relationship disappears when RSAFS is flat or declining.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while UNRATE is falling, the economy is expanding with improving utilization and earnings leverage, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter. The mechanism fails in stagflationary rebounds where production rises but unemployment does not improve.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint trigger does not produce positive 60d SPX returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or if the sign flips during expansionary periods.
